title: MIGRANT EDUCATION PROGRAM - 211info
text: HIGH DESERT EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT MIGRANT EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides funds to help state and local educational agencies remove barriers to migrant students' school enrollment, attendance, and achievement. These services can include tutoring, individual help in class or after school, summer school, free 24-hour accident injury insurance, parent involvement support, referrals to community resources, and college and career transition support for older youth. 2804 SW 6th Street Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following criteria: -Age 21 and younger -Eligible for free public education through grade 12 and who have not yet graduated or obtained a GED -Moved in the preceding 36 months due to economic necessity from one residence to another and from one school district to another OR to accompany or join a parent, guardian, or spouse who is a migratory agricultural worker or migratory fisher -Must have engaged in new qualifying temporary or seasonal work in agriculture or fishing within 60 days of the move -Must have employment of less than 12 months duration in agricultural or fishing industries Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 693-5600 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 693-5600 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Deschutes, Jefferson, and Wheeler County Services: Migrant Education Programs Specialized Information and Referral * Migrants Other Services Offered At This Location: EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - HIGH DESERT HOME SCHOOL OFFICE REHABILITATION/HABILITATION SERVICES - EARLY INTERVENTION/EARLY CHILDHOOD SPECIAL EDUCATION If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. HIGH DESERT EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT
